skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec. Use for: programmatic video generation, animated graphics, motion design, data-driven videos, React animations to video. Triggers: remotion, render video from code, tsx to video, react video, programmatic video, remotion render, code to video, animated video, motion graphics code, react animation video
skill.summary: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports ...
skill.author: inference-sh-8

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' patterns are markdown code fences in documentation, not actual shell execution. The 'pipe to shell' pattern is the standard documented install method for the inference.sh CLI with SHA-256 checksum verification. Network URLs are legitimate service integrations (inference.sh, remotion.dev). The skill makes API calls to inference.sh to render videos - this is intended functionality.

content.value_statement: Generate professional videos programmatically from React/Remotion TSX code. Pass component code, get MP4 output with full animation support including springs, sequences, and interpolation.
actual_capabilities: Render MP4 videos from React/Remotion TSX component code
Support all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence
Configure video output: resolution, FPS, duration, codec
Pass dynamic props to components for data-driven videos
limitations: Requires inference.sh API access for rendering
Complex animations may require longer render times
Audio support limited to basic codec options
No built-in asset management for external images/videos

use_cases.title: Data-Driven Marketing Videos
use_cases.description: Generate personalized video content from data: dynamic titles, animated counters, product showcases

use_cases.title: Developer Tutorial Animations
use_cases.description: Create code tutorial videos with animated syntax highlighting, code reveal effects, and dynamic diagrams

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Automation
use_cases.description: Automate video creation for social platforms with custom animations, text overlays, and branding

prompt_templates.title: Simple Fade Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a video with fading text 'Hello World' on a dark background. Use 30fps, 3 seconds duration, 1920x1080 resolution.

prompt_templates.title: Animated Counter
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a loading animation with a counter that goes from 0 to 100%. Use spring physics for smooth animation at 60fps.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Sequence Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video with three text elements that fade in sequentially: 'First', 'Second', 'Third'. Each appears for 1 second.

prompt_templates.title: Data Visualization
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a bar chart animation showing data values [{value: 30}, {value: 70}, {value: 50}]. Bars should animate from 0 to their values.

output_examples.input: Render a video with a bouncing ball animation using spring physics
output_examples.output: A 5-second MP4 video at 60fps showing a white ball bouncing on a purple background with realistic spring physics

output_examples.input: Create an animated title card with company branding
output_examples.output: A 3-second intro video with company logo scaling in, tagline fading up, and smooth background gradient animation

best_practices: Keep animations under 10 seconds for faster rendering
Use interpolation for smooth value transitions instead of manual frame calculations
Test code in Remotion Studio before using in production
Pass props for dynamic content rather than hardcoding values
anti_patterns: Avoid complex 3D transformations that require additional libraries
Do not embed large base64 images in code - use external URLs instead
Avoid rendering extremely long videos (>60 seconds) in a single pass

faq.question: What is Remotion?
faq.answer: Remotion is a framework for programmatically creating videos using React. It allows you to write video components in TypeScript/React and render them to MP4.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: You need the inference.sh CLI installed. Run: curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh

faq.question: Can I use external images or audio?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use the Img and Audio components to include external assets via URL.

faq.question: How long does rendering take?
faq.answer: Simple animations (3-5 seconds) typically render in 30-60 seconds. Complex videos may take several minutes.

faq.question: What video formats are supported?
faq.answer: The default output is MP4. You can specify codec in the input parameters.

faq.question: Can I render multiple compositions?
faq.answer: Yes, specify different composition_id values in your input to render multiple videos in sequence.
